title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

items.title: May 29: Floor confirmation at 0.0. METHODOLOGY RULING: GENDER-APARTHEID-FORMAL-RECOGNITION formalized as independent 0.0-floor-qualifying category (Decree No. 18 May 14 removing minimum marriage age; People's Tribunal Dec 2025 crimes-against-humanity finding).

items.title: May 26: Three May 26 discrete crackdown events (Izmir tear gas, Güzelbahçe mayor detained, 93 social media blocks) all pattern-continuation; fail categorical-novelty test from May 25 ruling. Documented hold consistent with May 27 carry-forward.
items.content_text: Three May 26 discrete crackdown events (Izmir tear gas, Güzelbahçe mayor detained, 93 social media blocks) all pattern-continuation; fail categorical-novelty test from May 25 ruling. Documented hold consistent with May 27 carry-forward.

items.title: May 25: Slovakia 33.6 → 31.6 (Δ -2.0): European Parliament 418-207 Votes to Urge EU Conditionality Mechanism — New Tier-1.5 Methodology Category
items.content_text: One formal score change tonight: Slovakia moves from 33.6 to 31.6 (Δ -2.0, Developing band sustained) following the European Parliament's 418-207 vote on May 20, 2026, formally urging the European Commission to trigger the EU Rule of Law Conditionality Mechanism — the same instrument that froze €10.4 billion in Hungarian EU funds under Orbán. The vote was backed by a cross-group supermajority (EPP, S&D, Renew Europe, Greens), citing the dissolution of Slovakia's special prosecution office, cuts…

items.title: May 24: Turkey 17.6 → 15.1 (Δ -2.5): Riot Police Physically Seize CHP Headquarters — Second-Event-at-Floor Apply
items.content_text: Two formal score applies in a normal-nightly cycle following yesterday's high-apply event. Turkey moves from 17.6 to 15.1 (Δ -2.5, remaining Critical) after Turkish riot police stormed and physically occupied CHP party headquarters on May 24 — an event qualitatively distinct from the May 21 judicial removal of CHP leadership that drove yesterday's -4.9. The second apply uses the new second-event-at-floor convention: when an entity has just received a formal apply at Critical-band proximity and a…

items.title: May 23: BAND CROSSING — Turkey 22.5 → 17.6 (Δ -4.9): Judicial Removal of Opposition Party Leadership Triggers Critical Reclassification
items.content_text: Two band crossings and four formal score applies in a single normal-nightly cycle — the highest formal-apply count since the May 21 WIDE event. Turkey's downgrade (22.5 to 17.6, Developing to Critical) is triggered by the Turkish appeals court's annulment of the CHP party congress on May 21, the judicial removal of opposition leader Özgür Özel and the entire CHP leadership, followed by police detention of 13 people on May 23 and an Istanbul stock-market circuit-breaker. The event establishes a n…

items.title: May 22: Slovakia -5.5: Second EU 'Dismantler' Downgrade in Two Nights — Magnitude Scales with Starting Position
items.content_text: Normal nightly cycle following the May 21 WIDE event. Slovakia's Liberties.eu 'Dismantler' classification — applied to Croatia at -7.8 one night earlier — now produces a -5.5 formal downgrade for Slovakia, establishing that Dismantler magnitude scales with starting position rather than applying uniformly. Microsoft's Azure/Unit 8200 accountability arc (GM firing, services termination) generates the benchmark's first dual-signal weighting case at severity: ACC dimension improves on the accountabi…

items.title: May 21: Hungary +6.3: The Benchmark's First Formal Upgrade — Structural Governance Reversal Scores as Compassion Capacity
items.content_text: May 21 is the benchmark's widest single cycle: 49 entities assessed, 4 formal score changes applied, 2 upward band crossings applied, 21 first-baselines established, and the symmetric-evaluation rule — that positive evidence is weighted equally with negative — validated for the first time at the formal-change level. Hungary's +6.3 upgrade is historic: the first time the benchmark has moved a score upward on positive governance evidence. Mongolia's +6.3 follows immediately, validated by UN High C…

items.title: May 20: Anthropic at Exact 60.0 Boundary: Band-Crossing Proposed Upward, Pending DC Circuit Ruling
items.content_text: May 20 is the highest event-density night of the current cycle: three forward signals from May 19 all resolved. Meta executed its 7,900-person layoff — and the benchmark discovered surveillance software had been deployed on employee laptops during the pre-notice period. An Amazon warehouse worker died on April 6; management directed workers to continue around the body for over an hour; the death went unreported for a week. Microsoft fired its Israel Country Manager after Unit 8200 used Azure to…

items.title: May 19: Palestine Floor-Designation Evidence Accumulating Across a Band Boundary
items.content_text: May 19 is a pre-event baseline cycle: 17 entities confirmed, 0 formal score changes, 8 sub-threshold movements documented. The most consequential finding is editorial: Palestine sits at composite 25.0 (Developing), but the accumulated evidence — OHCHR-documented ban of 37 aid groups, ongoing killings during the ceasefire, and 77% of the population in acute food insecurity — matches the pattern the methodology applies for floor designation. The benchmark logs this as an active methodology-evoluti…

items.title: May 18: China Crosses Critical Boundary: Band Crossing Developing → Critical (20.3 → 19.5)
items.content_text: May 18 is the cycle's first band-crossing night since May 15. The China assessment is the critical decision: a -0.8 delta that crosses from Developing into Critical (20.3 → 19.5) via a quarter-step BND dock for state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply — a methodology category not yet formalized. The EU's 20th sanctions package (April 23) designated 16 Chinese entities for dual-use supply to Russia's military-industrial complex, the first-ever such designation. Apply=true was s…

items.title: May 17: Israel IDP Camp Strike Pattern: 96 Killed Overnight, Parents and Children Together
items.content_text: May 17 is a sub-threshold cycle: no formal score changes cross the 5-point application threshold, but the day is analytically dense. The headline finding is the India-Pakistan Indus Waters Treaty bifurcation — a single international ruling (PCA Supplemental Award, May 15) scores opposite for two adjacent entities on the same day. India formally rejected the award as 'null and void,' producing a -1.5 documented downgrade (28.1 → 26.6) on a new ACC-dimension category: deliberate-non-compliance-wit…

items.title: May 16: Oracle First-Baseline: 6 a.m. Email Termination of 30,000 Workers — Boundary at 20.6
items.content_text: May 16 is a backfill cycle covering a single calendar day. The most consequential finding is Oracle's first-baseline assessment at 20.6 — proposed -7.8 from the published 28.4, landing 0.6pt above the Critical/Developing boundary. The 6 a.m. email termination of up to 30,000 workers (concurrent with a 95% net income jump and a $26M CFO stock award) is the heaviest single EMP-dimension event in the Fortune 500 cluster this assessment year, and anchors three new methodology categories: algorithmic…

items.title: May 15: Anthropic Boundary-Down: Safety Pledge Reversal Under Government Pressure — First Canonical Instance
items.content_text: May 15 was the methodology's most consequential single-night hold-release cycle: three major entities (Anthropic, Microsoft, Google/Alphabet) came off hold simultaneously, generating five score changes, ten new conduct categories, and four boundary-watch flags. The defining event is Anthropic's RSP v3 pre-deployment safety pledge drop (February 26) — the first cleanly documented 'safety-pledge-reversal-under-government-pressure' in the AI labs cluster, producing a boundary-direction downgrade fr…

items.title: May 14: xAI Third Governance Failure Category — Same-Day Event
items.content_text: May 14 produced the richest single-night methodology harvest in the benchmark's history: nine new conduct categories documented across fourteen assessments, including the first 'medical-science-denial enforcement' designation (Senegal charging patients with undetectable viral loads for 'voluntary HIV transmission'), the first 'aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding' (South Sudan's famine trajectory amplified by US aid cuts), and the first instance of a partial external-accountability-reversal su…

items.title: May 13: Hungary MAGYAR-ERA FIRST OPERATIONAL BASELINE — BAND CROSSING: Developing to Functional (37.5 → 41.4). Cabinet decentralized to 16 ministries with 3 new standalone portfolios (health, environment, education); comprehensive audit of ministries and state-owned companies ordered; payment suspension beyond normal operational management enacted; first cabinet meeting held May 13 with drought-response expert consultation and emergency measures. ICC arrest-warrant enforcement reversal credited on INT dimension. Conservative-rounding discount applied from full math reconstruction (43.0) to boundary-case-respecting 41.4. 30-day re-assessment June 9.

items.title: May 12: Pakistan BAND CROSSING — Developing to Critical. HRCP 2025 annual report: 273+ enforced disappearances; 531,700 Afghans coerced out; Ahmadi minority killings; 6,500+ GBV cases; 145/148 Gender Gap. Conservative proposed composite 17.5 (precise math 17.2). Delta sub-threshold but band crossings are always material.

items.title: May 11: 72-hour ceasefire methodology held cleanly — Russia's 6-event breach record complete with new 'strategic-format-exploitation' conduct category. New Day-3 Sustained-Conduct rule articulated (methodology v1.2). 4-day sequential backfill complete; series is current.
items.content_text: Compassion Benchmark daily briefing for 2026-05-11.

items.title: May 10: Day-2 ceasefire confirms bad-faith inverted-signal methodology — Russia's cumulative breach record spans 5 events. UNAIDS 25.6% HIV visit drop strengthens Senegal band crossing. Pacific boundary cluster identified: Timor-Leste, Kiribati, Marshall Islands all at 39.1 (0.9 below Functional floor).
items.content_text: Compassion Benchmark daily briefing for 2026-05-10.

items.title: May 9: Hungary — HOLD RELEASED. Peter Magyar sworn in May 9. First-day enacted actions: EU flag flown at Parliament (SYS/BND); TV2 news director and pro-Orbán presenters dismissed (ACC/SYS); Sulyok dismissal demanded with May 31 hard deadline (ACC); €17B frozen EU funds named top priority with 27 EU rule-of-law supermilestones agreed pre-inauguration (ACC/BND/INT); inaugural address: 'system change / service not rule' framing. Five dimensions credited +0.5 each. Proposed 37.5 (Developing). First positive EU-cluster signal this rotation. Largest single-cycle positive delta in countries index this rotation.
items.content_text: Hungary — HOLD RELEASED. Peter Magyar sworn in May 9. First-day enacted actions: EU flag flown at Parliament (SYS/BND); TV2 news director and pro-Orbán presenters dismissed (ACC/SYS); Sulyok dismissal demanded with May 31 hard deadline (ACC); €17B frozen EU funds named top priority with 27 EU rule-of-law supermilestones agreed pre-inauguration (ACC/BND/INT); inaugural address: 'system change / service not rule' framing. Five dimensions credited +0.5 each. Proposed 37.5 (Developing). First positi…

items.title: May 8: Math-hygiene cluster extends to ai-labs index. India-Pakistan anniversary confirms structural entrenchment. Zero band changes. Zero floor designations. Myanmar hospital-targeting conduct documented.
items.content_text: Compassion Benchmark daily briefing for 2026-05-08.

items.title: May 7: Character.AI — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION. Consumer AI harm-by-design: Pennsylvania AG May 5 2026 first-of-its-kind enforcement action over chatbot medical impersonation; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number, offered clinical assessment to depressed user. January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death lawsuits including two minor children who died by suicide. 20M+ monthly active users; no model card, no system card, no third-party safety audit; reactive-only remediation pattern. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 23.8 → 0.
items.content_text: A 19-entity cycle produced four material downgrade proposals and the second consecutive floor designation in as many days. Character.AI (composite 23.8 → 0, developing → critical/floor) is the first floor entity in the cluster whose designation rests on consumer-product harm-by-design: chatbots impersonating licensed psychiatrists at scale, a five-lawsuit wrongful death settlement involving two minor fatalities, and a structural absence of proactive safety architecture at 20M+ user scale. Slovak…

items.title: May 6: Ghost Robotics first agent baseline — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION proposed. Robotics-labs index first floor entity. Cedra AI April 13 2026 ethics analysis documents explicit refusal to restrict military payload use ('we don't know what they do with them'); refusal to sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signed by 6 peer firms; sniper-rifle-equipped quadruped deployment to US-Mexico border. No model card, system card, safety policy, or third-party red-teaming for any product. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 9.4 → 0.
items.content_text: A 14-entity priority cycle produced three material downgrade proposals and the first floor designation in the robotics-labs index. Ghost Robotics (composite 9.4 → 0) was designated floor on its first baseline, establishing a precedent: explicit policy refusal to restrict military payload use — without a documented mass-casualty event — crosses the floor threshold. Waymo (48.4 → 35.9, functional → developing) and Greece (46.9 → 39.1, functional → developing) both produced first-baseline band cros…

items.title: May 5: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 5
items.content_text: Math-hygiene cluster grew from 3 to 8 entities in one cycle, now spanning all five bands — bidirectional discrepancies rule out a single formula error; bulk data-team audit overdue.

items.title: May 4: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 4
items.content_text: Alphabet/Google: 600+ employees revolt against Pentagon classified-AI deal on the same day Fortune documents the governance regression from Project Maven — assessed composite moves to 37.4 (-3.2), sub-threshold but the most evidentially significant finding of the cycle.

items.title: May 3: Botswana confirmed at 67.5 Established following first-baseline assessment. The dominant driver is the April 28–30, 2026 full repeal of colonial-era Penal Code provisions that had criminalized same-sex relationships with up to seven years' imprisonment — the legislative codification of rights established by the 2019 High Court ruling and 2021 Court of Appeal upholding. UNAIDS applauded the repeal publicly on April 30. President Duma Boko — a human-rights lawyer who previously litigated LEGABIBO and Kanane LGBTQI cases — provides strong INT and SYS continuity. EQU is the dominant dimension driver at +0.7. Delta of +6.6 is material; band remains Established.
items.content_text: Botswana confirmed at 67.5 Established following first-baseline assessment. UNAIDS applauded the repeal publicly on April 30. President Duma Boko — a human-rights lawyer who previously litigated LEGABIBO…

items.title: May 2: Mauritius confirmed at 60.0 Functional following first-baseline assessment. Band crossed from Established to Functional driven by recurring accountability gaps: failure to present required Periodic Report before the African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights at two consecutive sessions (Nov 2024, Oct 2025); multi-decade Chagossian rights deficit (BIOT court rulings Mar–Apr 2026 affirming right of abode highlight unresolved displacement harm); and civil-liberties concerns from mandatory SIM re-registration and Nov 2024 election social media block. Partially offset by 2023 Supreme Court sodomy-law decriminalization and Dec 2024 State of the Economy audit transparency. Low confidence first-baseline; band crossing confirmed by score-updater.
items.content_text: Mauritius confirmed at 60.0 Functional following first-baseline assessment. items.title: May 1: Anthropic moved from Established to Functional (61.6 → 59.7) based on two new findings: the White House blocked Anthropic's plan to expand its Mythos AI tool to approximately 70 organizations, and the Mythos security breach remained without comprehensive public remediation disclosure 24 days after the incident. The Established → Functional band crossing is significant despite the modest composite delta. Anthropic's sustained refusal to accept Pentagon autonomous-weapons contracts — at material economic cost — is the primary mitigating factor limiting the depth of this adjustment.

The Established → Functional band crossing is significant despite the modest composite delta. Anthropic's sustained refusal to accept Pentagon autonomous-weapons contracts — at material e…

items.title: Apr 30: Pentagon classified AI deal signed April 27-28 despite 580+ employee letter (incl. 100+ DeepMind researchers, 20+ directors/VPs signed openly). Senior DeepMind scientist publicly 'incredibly ashamed.' Compounds Feb 2025 weapons-pledge removal. Same-week $100M drone swarm exit is partial offset. Established → Functional band crossing.
